title: Delay Movement when backlash compensation
author: hanifhan
body:
hi , ive been trying to use a backlash compensation , but i found out there is kind of delay ( accel/deccel ) movement when the machine do a compensation backlash , so the movement became not smooth , is it designed like that or any explanation on it , because it will impact in machining time  ?

machining time when use backlash compensation : 13 minutes and 40 second
machining time when not using backlash compensation : 10 minutes  and 20 second

title: why only 1 step jog motion is not posible ?
author: MAK-1966
body:
hello dear forum,

I am trying to move the step motor 1 step with the $J= X0.001---- jog command
however grbl is accumulating the jog commands
and moves 0.010 when the total of 1 step jog commands is 10

I want to move 1 step exact (0.001) with jog command

is this not posible ?

thank you

title: Problems when working via ethernet
author: zab-ig
body:
If any error occurs, for example, a limit switch has tripped or an unknown command in the r-code, a message appears:
ALARM:1
[MSG:Reset to continue]
But if you press the reset button, the controller stops responding. Only restarting the controller helps.
When working through uart, there is no such problem. 

При возникновении любой ошибки, например сработал концевой выключатель или неизвестная команда в г-коде, появляется сообщение: 
ALARM:1
[MSG:Reset to continue]
Но если нажать кнопку ресет, контроллер перестает отвечать. Помогает только перезагрузка контроллера.
При работе через юарт такой проблемы нет.

title: Laser support?
author: Edocecrous
body:
Hi,
I couldn't find any means to contact you, i hope i'm not intruding by placing this under 'issues', even though it's more like a feature request.
I'm replacing my Arduino UNO + cnc shield in my laser with Arduino MEGA + Ramps 1.4, but i would be much happier with GRBL-Advanced and an STM board. And was wondering if there is a possibility to include the 'laser' feature of the Arduino GRBL version.

title: What is the point of external EEPROM?
author: MarkHerhold
body:
Hi there, 

Just found this project. Great code quality. I purchased a STM32F411 and was able to compile and load the software on my Mac and it works!

I noticed that there's support for an external EEPROM module but I don't know why we would need an external one. Maybe to prevent overwrites between flashes?

title: Does Grbl-Advanced use the grbl stepper "on" time setting?
author: jdeline
body:
This is less of an "issue" and more of a question.  In line number 118 of "stepper.c", the following variable is declared:

uint8_t step_pulse_time;  // Step pulse reset time after step rise

I cannot find where the variable gets assigned, nor where it gets used in the program.  How does GA set the stepper "on" time?

Do you want to create a "Discussions" section in this repository for questions like this?
Thanks!

title: CNC spindle motor PWM signal goes off (zero speed) when Backlash compensation makes a correction.
author: lairdgit
body:
I downloaded this version source:
#define GRBL_VERSION            "1.1i"
Revision 2
Schildkroet released this on Apr 15, 2020
---
I compiled it with EmBlitz 1.11.
I uploaded the code to a STM32 Nucleo F411RE using STM32 ST-LINK Utility v4.6.0.0

I have a garden variety CNC machine.
Using a dial indicator the backlash correction works great.

However, when using the CNC to mill some stock, the PWM signal goes to the inactive state when the backlash correction makes a correction.  The spindle motor is always slowing down and speeding up.

This may be great for a laser machine but not for a spindle motor.
Laird

title: Stepper Timing
author: ClockedLogic
body:
Hi SchildKroet,
Can you tell me what the valid values are for  F_TIMING_STEPPER in  https://github.com/Schildkroet/GRBL-Advanced/blob/software/grbl/util.h please.  I'm not certain what this should contain It seems to be 1/4 of the CPU clock where the existing value is 24000000 and the F_CPU is 96000000. 

CPU frequency on my board is 168MHz
does this mean I should set  F_CPU = 168000000 and F_TIMING_STEPPER = 42000000 ??

title: Targeting other STM32F4XX MCUs is there a how to?
author: ClockedLogic
body: I cloned the main soruce for GRBL Advanced and created my own core for STMF405 as I couldn't see how to add support for it under the main project source tree . This is due to my lack of understanding of EmBitz and how to configure it.  How are other MCUs supported through the main source tree?

title: GCode Senders - What are you using?
author: ClockedLogic
body:
I have a version of UGS (Universal GCode Sender) that I modified to use the extra two axis (A and B)  for another project. Is anyone interested or are you happy with what you are using. 

I find UGS to be quite a good tool and recommend it . It needs Java 8 JRE or later and runs on most platforms, possibly even RPI but haven't tested it.
